claims.claim_text: Ollama supports pulling models locally and exposes a REST API for running and managing models.
claims.span_hint: section: Ollama: Best for Fast Entry
claims.rationale: The upstream API reference explicitly documents model pull endpoints and the REST API for running and managing local models.

claims.claim_text: `llama.cpp` is built for broad hardware portability and uses the GGUF format for local model execution.
claims.span_hint: section: `llama.cpp`: Best for Portability and Direct Control
claims.rationale: The upstream README states that `llama.cpp` targets a wide range of hardware and that models must be stored in GGUF format.

claims.claim_text: `llama.cpp` includes `llama-server`, a lightweight OpenAI-compatible HTTP server for local serving.
claims.rationale: The upstream README directly documents `llama-server` as a lightweight OpenAI-compatible HTTP server.

claims.claim_text: vLLM is a serving-oriented inference engine with high-throughput features such as continuous batching, prefix caching, and an OpenAI-compatible API server.
claims.span_hint: section: vLLM: Best for Learning Real Serving Dynamics
claims.rationale: The upstream README explicitly lists serving throughput, continuous batching, prefix caching, and an OpenAI-compatible API server.

claims.claim_text: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework that includes structured outputs and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
claims.span_hint: section: sglang: Best for Advanced Workflow-Oriented Serving
claims.rationale: The upstream README describes SGLang as a high-performance serving framework and lists structured outputs plus OpenAI API compatibility among its core features.

further_reading.title: Ollama API Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers local model pulling, generation, and service endpoints for learner-scale setups.

further_reading.title: llama.cpp README
further_reading.why_relevant: Documents GGUF workflows, hardware backends, CLI usage, and the built-in local server.

further_reading.title: vLLM README
further_reading.why_relevant: Summarizes the serving-oriented features that distinguish vLLM from simple local runners.

further_reading.title: SGLang README
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the advanced serving features, structured outputs, and API compatibility discussed in the module.

notes: Excluded quiz items, exercise steps, analogies, and non-load-bearing pedagogical advice. Seeded only the upstream-verifiable tool capability claims that would benefit from inline citations.
